name: Stoor Worm
description: The Stoor Worm, also known as the Mester Stoor Worm, is a gigantic and malevolent sea serpent or dragon from Orcadian mythology. It is said to be so large that its body encircles the entire world. This monstrous creature is responsible for devastation and plagues, possessing a venomous breath that can wither crops and poison the land. Its insatiable appetite drives it to devour ships, entire villages, and even islands. Only a hero of immense courage and cunning can hope to defeat the Stoor Worm and save the world from its wrath. The Stoor Worm's existence is a constant threat, a symbol of the destructive forces of nature and the ever-present danger lurking beneath the waves. Legends say it can only be appeased by the sacrifice of seven virgins every Saturday, highlighting the terror it instilled in the hearts of the Orcadian people. The creature is a formidable opponent, possessing immense strength and resilience, making it a truly terrifying figure in Orcadian folklore. Its defeat often requires a clever trick or a powerful weapon, reflecting the importance of intelligence and resourcefulness in overcoming seemingly insurmountable odds. The Stoor Worm serves as a cautionary tale, reminding people of the destructive potential of unchecked power and the importance of bravery in the face of adversity.
subtitle: Gigantic, venomous sea serpent of Orcadian legend.
appearance: The Stoor Worm is depicted as an enormous sea serpent or dragon, so large that its body can encircle the world. Its scales are said to be dark and impenetrable, and its eyes glow with a malevolent green light. It possesses a massive head with rows of sharp teeth and a long, forked tongue that drips with venom. Some accounts describe it as having multiple heads, further enhancing its terrifying appearance. Its movements create massive waves and whirlpools, and its presence is often accompanied by a foul stench.
habitat: The Stoor Worm inhabits the depths of the ocean surrounding the Orkney Islands, lurking beneath the waves and occasionally surfacing to wreak havoc on the land. Its lair is said to be a vast underwater cave filled with the treasures it has accumulated from sunken ships and devoured villages.
behavior: The Stoor Worm is a highly aggressive and destructive creature, driven by an insatiable hunger and a desire to spread chaos. It attacks ships and coastal settlements without warning, devouring everything in its path. It is also said to possess a cruel intelligence, delighting in the suffering of others. The Stoor Worm is a symbol of the destructive forces of nature and the ever-present threat of the unknown.

keyMyths.title: The Defeat of the Stoor Worm
keyMyths.description: Assipattle, a young boy, uses a hot peat to burn the Stoor Worm's liver, causing it to explode and die. This act creates the Orkney and Shetland Islands.

sources.author: Ernest Marwick
sources.work: The Folklore of Orkney and Shetland
sources.description: A comprehensive collection of Orcadian and Shetland folklore, including tales of the Stoor Worm.
sources.text: A comprehensive collection of Orcadian and Shetland folklore, including tales of the Stoor Worm.

sources.author: Walter Traill Dennison
